published_rows_all.summary: When setting up an account, users can choose to receive a verification code via email or an authenticator app.
published_rows_all.action_text: Choose to receive verification code via email or an authenticator app
published_rows_all.source_quote: I think when you set up your account, it asks to choose either from getting the code on e-mail or use an authenticator app.

published_rows_all.summary: Users can choose to receive notifications via email or the application.

published_rows_all.summary: Users should refresh their email if they are having trouble accessing their Bitwarden account, ensuring they use the email associated with the Bitwarden account.
published_rows_all.condition_text: If users have trouble accessing Bitwarden, or their login information is defaulting to an incorrect email address.
published_rows_all.recommendation_text: Refresh your email and verify it is the email address associated with your Bitwarden account. Store login information within Bitwarden rather than the Edge browser.
published_rows_all.source_quote: I would suggest maybe refreshing your e-mail clay. And see if it comes in. It's got to be whatever e-mail you use to create your bitwarden account.

published_rows_all.summary: To export passwords from Microsoft Edge and import them into Bitwarden, access the Edge wallet settings app.
published_rows_all.condition_text: Passwords are saved in Microsoft Edge
published_rows_all.action_text: Access the Edge wallet settings app to export passwords and import them into Bitwarden.
published_rows_all.source_quote: export our passwords that are in Microsoft Edge and import them into bitwarden

published_rows_all.summary: Export passwords from Microsoft Edge to import into Bitwarden by navigating to edge://settings/passwords, exporting passwords to a file, and saving the file in a secure location.
published_rows_all.action_text: Export passwords from Microsoft Edge and save the exported file in a secure location for importing into Bitwarden.

published_rows_all.summary: To change emails, export your vault from settings using the browser extension, then import the exported CSV file into your personal email vault.
published_rows_all.condition_text: When changing emails and using the browser extension
published_rows_all.action_text: Export the vault to a CSV file and import into the new account.
published_rows_all.source_quote: export it from here and then you can import the file you exported on your personal e-mail

published_rows_all.summary: To import data to a vault, navigate to tools, then import data from the vault, selecting a destination folder and the correct file format (CSV).
published_rows_all.action_text: Navigate to tools, then import data from the vault, selecting a destination folder and the correct file format (CSV).
